description: svi2-pro render presets. params map 1:1 to validate_render_params keys (worker-consumable + UI-selectable). Omit motion_scale_schedule/motion_scale_t to keep RoPE motion scaling off (1.0).

presets.label: SVI 2.0 Pro canonical (reference-faithful)
presets.description: Matches the official Stable-Video-Infinity 2.0 Pro inference recipe exactly: RAW previous-clip latent handoff (pixel_re_encode OFF — Pro forbids decode/re-encode), trained 480p budget at 832x480, clean concat+overlap-trim stitch (no crossfade), no ref-pad crutch, no AdaIN. Identity holds via anchor latent + raw motion latent + the error-recycling SVI LoRA. Use ONE prompt across all clips and keep appearance-change verbs OUT of it (the anchor defines identity; the prompt drives MOTION). This is the recommended baseline for long coherent chains.
presets.notes: VALIDATED 2026-06-05: 5x69f (overlap 5) -> 325f @16fps -> RIFE 48 = 20.3s; demonic-nun identity HELD across all 5 clips (no demon->human drift), even with a large multi-action prompt. Reference CLI: --num_overlap_frame 5 --num_motion_latent 1 --cfg_scale 4.0, 50 steps, 832x480, 4n+1 frames. VRAM: blocks_to_swap=40 = safe ~10 GiB peak; blocks_to_swap=28 is faster (~30 s/step) when you have >=4 GiB free headroom, but at <2 GiB free Windows (no expandable_segments) thrashes the allocator and slows ~3x. Pair with env SVI2_FP8_COMPUTE=bf16 + SVI2_ATTENTION=flash2 (flash_attn NOT optional per SVI tips). For length, raise num_clips (stitched = frames_per_clip + (num_clips-1)*(frames_per_clip-num_overlap_frame)). For transformation (not just motion) keep identity via an edited keyframe as --ref-image (edit-then-animate), NOT via ICN or appearance-change prompts.

presets.label: Natural (reference-match)
presets.description: Vanilla Wan2.2-I2V dynamics: no RoPE motion scaling, native 16fps, default shift, full 50 steps. Light AdaIN + pixel re-encode for continuation. Matches the coherent motion other Wan2.2 i2v pipelines produce. Recommended baseline.
presets.notes: blocks_to_swap=0 needs ~26 GiB VRAM (DGX/128G). For a 16GB card raise blocks_to_swap. For smoother high-fps use the natural-rife48 preset (adds frames, keeps motion speed).

presets.label: Natural + RIFE 48fps
presets.description: Same natural Wan2.2-I2V render at native 16fps, then frame-interpolated to 48fps. Adds in-between frames (no motion speed-up) for buttery playback. Uses rife-ncnn-vulkan if rife_bin is set, else falls back to ffmpeg minterpolate.
presets.notes: interpolate_method=rife needs rife_bin (path to rife-ncnn-vulkan); without it set interpolate_method=ffmpeg. Render cost identical to natural-reference; interpolation is a fast post-step.

presets.label: Forced motion 24fps (over-driven)
presets.description: Aggressive motion via per-clip RoPE ramp + lowered shift + 24fps + stronger AdaIN, fewer steps. Produces exaggerated/uncanny movement (head-whip, face-morph) and 1.5x-fast playback vs native 16fps. Kept for A/B reference; NOT the natural look.
presets.notes: motion_scale_schedule > 1.5 drives RoPE temporal stretch past training distribution -> deformation. Diagnostic only.

presets.label: Natural (reference-match) — 16GB
presets.description: natural-reference render targeted at a 16GB card. Identical diffusion params; offloads all 40 DiT blocks to CPU (blocks_to_swap=40) so only the active block + VAE peak resides on GPU. Same quality as natural-reference, slower per step from the CPU<->GPU block traffic.
presets.notes: 16GB recipe: blocks_to_swap=40 (full DiT block swap; VAE auto-offloads to CPU during diffusion). Also run with env SVI2_FP8_COMPUTE=bf16 (Blackwell scaled_mm colour fix) and SVI2_ATTENTION=sdpa|flash2 — those are operator env levers, not preset params. If still OOM at 832x480, drop width/height.

presets.label: Natural + RIFE 48fps — 16GB
presets.description: natural-rife48 render targeted at a 16GB card. Identical diffusion + RIFE interpolation; offloads all 40 DiT blocks to CPU (blocks_to_swap=40). RIFE interpolation is a post-render step and adds no GPU peak to the diffusion phase.
presets.notes: 16GB recipe: blocks_to_swap=40 (full DiT block swap; VAE auto-offloads to CPU). Pair with env SVI2_FP8_COMPUTE=bf16 + SVI2_ATTENTION. interpolate_method=rife needs rife_bin or rife_torch; else set interpolate_method=ffmpeg.

presets.label: Forced motion 24fps (over-driven) — 16GB
presets.description: forced-motion-24 diagnostic render targeted at a 16GB card. Identical over-driven RoPE/AdaIN params; offloads all 40 DiT blocks to CPU (blocks_to_swap=40). Diagnostic look (head-whip/face-morph, 1.5x-fast) is unchanged — this only changes where the weights live.
presets.notes: 16GB recipe: blocks_to_swap=40 (full DiT block swap; VAE auto-offloads to CPU). Diagnostic only — motion_scale_schedule > 1.5 deforms past training distribution. Pair with env SVI2_FP8_COMPUTE=bf16 + SVI2_ATTENTION.

presets.label: FLF2V morph (start -> end keyframe) — 16GB
presets.description: First-last-frame morph. Animates a gradual, prompt-guided transition from --ref-image to --last-image over one seamless clip (no chaining seam). Pin the target state as an edited keyframe (e.g. Qwen-Image-Edit: same identity, changed appearance) and let the model interpolate. Works with stock Wan2.2-I2V-A14B weights via Kijai-style end-frame conditioning + mask. cfg 5.0, no ICN (the end keyframe drives the change, not conditioning noise). 65 frames at 960x544, RIFE to 48fps.
presets.notes: Requires --last-image (target end keyframe) at run time; --ref-image is the start frame. 16GB: blocks_to_swap=40, peak ~11 GiB, ~40 min for 65 frames on an RTX 5070 Ti. Single clip so num_clips/frames_per_clip set the morph duration (frames_per_clip must be 4n+1). For a longer morph raise frames_per_clip (81), or chain a follow-on clip with the same end-state ref. Keep ICN at 0 — the end frame already supplies the target.

presets.label: Chained long take, single prompt — 16GB
presets.description: Seamless long take the SVI way: ONE prompt across every clip (SVI does not drift/forget with a single prompt) plus crossfade-blended seams. Each clip continues the previous via the re-encoded motion tail; the cosine crossfade over num_overlap_frame hides the join. Use a rich, single descriptive prompt and let it run for as many clips as you want length. 960x544, RIFE to 48fps.
presets.notes: Single prompt is the point — supply ONE prompt file line; every clip reuses it (changing prompts per clip is what causes the cold-turkey switch). Length scales with num_clips: stitched frames = frames_per_clip + (num_clips-1)*(frames_per_clip-num_overlap_frame). 16GB: blocks_to_swap=40, peak ~11.4 GiB, ~2h45 for 4x65f on an RTX 5070 Ti (~40 min/clip). For smoother joins nudge num_overlap_frame up a little (6-8) and num_motion_latent to 2 (with num_motion_frame>=5), but keep them small or continuity over-constrains and the motion freezes.
